title: Undo, Restore, Revert, Reset, and Reflog Recovery · Git Stakeholder Mission
summary: A release owner discovers a secret-like fixture and incorrect commit after a tag candidate. Protect credentials, choose safe undo layers, recover a lost commit, and publish a reproducible replacement release plan. Planned artifact: Deliver Release and Recovery Exercise with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace ignore sources, directory scope, pattern grammar, negation, tracked-state limits, and check-ignore evidence.
Use attributes for text normalization, diff, merge, export, and binary behavior with check-attr evidence.
Control repository and working-tree line endings while separating attributes from core.autocrlf and reviewing renormalization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

What must be established before implementation?

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Reflog Object Recovery structural check is only one gate. an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: Name the Reflog Object Recovery precondition or invariant that the project case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer. Competency probe: Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Reflog entries are shared remote backups that never expire.

steps.title: Test an acceptance boundary
steps.instruction:
project case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer. Competency probe: Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

stimulus.title: Reflog Object Recovery failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Reflog entries are shared remote backups that never expire.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Reflog Object Recovery condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Reflog Object Recovery output and hide the violated an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Reflog Object Recovery without its stated preconditions, omit the an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Reflog Object Recovery for an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.
